content.text:
{"id":"75264b85-a50a-4a46-ad8c-34cc9526097b","identifier":"FRG-225","title":"Bug: Characters use sprite names instead of display labels","description":"LLM sees sprite names (Amelia, Lucy, Ash) instead of display names (Maple, Sol, Nyx). Talk action shows 'Nyx talk → Amelia' instead of 'Nyx talk → Maple'.

## Root Cause

In AIEngine.js, nearbyCharacters sends `c.name` (sprite name) to the LLM. The LLM returns that name in `targetCharacter`. The lookup `characters.find(c => c.name === decision.targetCharacter)` works internally, but all user-facing display is wrong.

## Fix

1. Send `label` (display name) in the nearbyCharacters context to the LLM
2. LLM returns label in targetCharacter
3. Update the find() lookup to match on label
4. Ensure event log and speech bubbles use labels consistently

## Acceptance Criteria

* LLM prompt shows Maple/Sol/Nyx, not Amelia/Lucy/Ash
* Talk decisions in event log show display names
* Character lookup still works correctly","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-225/bug-characters-use-sprite-names-instead-of-display-labels","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-225-bug-characters-use-sprite-names-instead-of-display-labels","createdAt":"2026-03-07T17:41:12.072Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T17:49:00.540Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze what needs to be fixed:

1. In `AIEngine.js`, line 85:
```js
.map(c => ({ name: c.name, label: c.label, tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty), action: c.action }));
```
The `nearbyCharacters` map sends both `name` (sprite name) AND `label` (display name), but the user prompt on line 102 tells the LLM to use `character_name` for `targetCharacter`:
```js
`  { "action": "talk",     "targetCharacter": "character_name", "dialogue": "what you say", "targetDialogue": "their response", "description": "..." }`;


```

2. The lookup in `_applyDecision` at line 172 searches by `name`, but since the LLM receives both `name` and `label` in the context, it might return either one. I need to either make the prompt explicitly specify using the label, or remove the `name` field entirely so the LLM only has access to the display name.

The fix involves three coordinated changes: only include `label` in the `nearbyCharacters` context sent to the LLM, update the prompt to clarify that `targetCharacter` should be the display name, and change the lookup to search by `label` instead of `name`. The event logging already handles this correctly by using `character.label` for both speaker and target labels.

```

js
.map(c => ({ name: c.label, label: c.label, tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty), action: c.action }));
```

Actually, I should simplify this further—just send `label` without the redundant `name` field so there's no ambiguity about which identifier the LLM should use.

The user prompt also needs updating to clarify that `targetCharacter` expects the display label, not the internal sprite name. Then in `_applyDecision`, I'll change the lookup from finding by `name` to finding by `label` to match what the LLM returns.

I should also verify the event logging and speech bubble handling are already using labels correctly—looks like they are based on the existing code that references `character.label` and `target.label`.

Now I need to check Engine.js and scene.js to see if there's any other place where character names might be displayed instead of labels, particularly in how talk actions are rendered.

content.text: Now I understand the issue clearly. Let me also check the Engine.js and scene.js for how talk events are displayed:

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of what needs to be fixed. Let me analyze:

1. In `AIEngine.js`, `nearbyCharacters` map (line 85):
   ```js
   .map(c => ({ name: c.name, label: c.label, tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty), action: c.action }));
   ```
   This sends BOTH `name` (sprite name like "Amelia") AND `label` (display name like "Maple"). The LLM sees both.

2. In user prompt (line 102):
   The prompt template uses `"character_name"` as the placeholder, suggesting the LLM should reference the sprite `name` field, but since both `name` and `label` are provided in the context, there's ambiguity about which one the LLM will actually use.

3. In `_applyDecision` (line 172):
   The code looks up characters by matching against `decision.targetCharacter`, which means whatever the LLM outputs needs to match the actual `name` field for the lookup to work.

The real issue is that I should send only the display name (`label`) to the LLM in the `nearbyCharacters` context, and update the lookup logic to match against that instead. I'll map the characters to use `name: c.label` so the LLM sees the display names and returns those, then adjust the character lookup to search by `label` rather than `name`. I see the issue now—the map is sending both `name` and `label` to the LLM, which creates ambiguity about which field to return. The cleaner fix is to remove the `name` field entirely and only pass `label`, then update the lookup logic to match against `c.label` and adjust the prompt hint accordingly. This approach is clean and unambiguous—the LLM sees labels as display names, returns the label in `targetCharacter`, and we match characters by their label. I'm checking the rest of the codebase to make sure `character.name` is only used internally for sprite lookups, not in any user-facing displays, then I'll create the feature branch and implement these changes.

2. In user prompt (line 102): Change hint `"character_name"` to `"character_label"` to match the field the LLM will see.

3. In `_applyDecision` (line 172): Change `c.name === decision.targetCharacter` to `c.label === decision.targetCharacter`.

fix: send display labels to LLM and look up talk targets by label (FRG-225)

- Update prompt hint from character_name to character_label to match
- Change talk target lookup from c.name to c.label so the returned
  label resolves to the correct Character instance

EOF
)"

gh pr create --title "fix: send display labels to LLM and look up talk targets by label (FRG-225)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Removes sprite name (`Amelia/Lucy/Ash`) from `nearbyCharacters` context sent to LLM; now only sends `label` (`Maple/Sol/Nyx`)
- Updates the `talk` action prompt hint from `character_name` to `character_label` so the LLM knows which field to echo back
- Changes `_applyDecision` target lookup from `c.name` to `c.label` so the LLM's response resolves to the correct `Character` instance

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify LLM prompt context shows `label: "Maple"` etc., not `name: "Amelia"`
- [ ] Trigger a talk action; confirm event log shows display names (e.g. `Nyx talk → Maple`)
- [ ] Confirm character lookup still works and both initiator speech bubble and target dialogue appear correctly

Fixes: FRG-225

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/AIEngine.js` — three targeted fixes:
  1. `nearbyCharacters` map: removed `name: c.name` (sprite name), now sends only `label: c.label` so the LLM sees display names (`Maple/Sol/Nyx`)
  2. User prompt hint: `"character_name"` → `"character_label"` to match the field the LLM sees
  3. `_applyDecision` talk lookup: `c.name === decision.targetCharacter` → `c.label === decision.targetCharacter` so the returned label resolves correctly

## Key Decisions
- Kept internal timer/pending maps keyed on `c.name` (sprite name) — those are private engine state and don't need to change
- Event log and speech bubbles already used `character.label` / `target.label`, so no changes needed there

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-225-fix-character-display-names`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/7

  3. `_applyDecision` talk lookup: `c.name === decision.targetCharacter` → `c.label === decision.targetCharacter` so the returned label resolves correctly
